skill.name: aws-cost-optimizer
skill.description: Comprehensive AWS cost analysis and optimization recommendations using AWS CLI and Cost Explorer
skill.summary: Comprehensive AWS cost analysis and optimization recommendations using AWS CLI and Cost Explorer
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell and AWS CLI patterns, but they are documentation examples rather than hidden executable code. The main confirmed risk is that the skill encourages AWS account inventory queries and cost cleanup workflows, including deletion-oriented recommendations that require careful review. The hardcoded URLs are AWS documentation links, and no evidence of malicious command-and-control, prompt injection, or credential exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: AWS CLI Account Inventory Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides AWS CLI examples for Cost Explorer, EC2, CloudWatch, snapshots, and RDS. These commands are legitimate for cost analysis, but they can expose cloud inventory and cost data when run with privileged AWS credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly presented as AWS CLI examples and are not hidden executable code. The risk is still meaningful because they query real account resources and billing data.

medium_findings.title: Deletion-Oriented Cost Optimization Guidance
medium_findings.description: The workflow recommends deleting unattached volumes, releasing unused Elastic IPs, terminating idle instances, deleting old snapshots, and creating deletion scripts. This is common cost optimization advice, but it can cause data loss or outages without validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly recommends deletion and termination workflows, while also warning users to verify and use dry runs. This supports a medium operational risk rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to AWS pricing, Well-Architected, and Cost Explorer API documentation. These are benign reference links and do not show evidence of data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to official AWS documentation pages. No suspicious endpoint or instruction to transmit user data was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several AWS CLI examples use shell date substitution to build time ranges. This is a normal shell pattern, but users should review commands before execution in credentialed AWS environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in documented shell examples and has no user-controlled interpolation. It remains relevant because copied commands execute against AWS accounts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Community Skill Self-Declares Safety
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill metadata labels itself as safe while the body includes commands and deletion workflows. This is not a prompt injection attempt, but marketplace risk should be based on audit results instead of self-declared metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The safety label is visible in metadata and conflicts with the operational risk of cleanup guidance. I did not find text attempting to override the auditor or skip review.

content.value_statement: AWS spending can grow through idle resources, missing tags, and inefficient instance choices. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through cost analysis, inventory checks, and practical savings recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Guides AWS Cost Explorer queries for service-level and daily cost analysis.
Provides AWS CLI examples for finding unattached EBS volumes and unused Elastic IPs.
Shows CloudWatch metric queries for EC2 and RDS utilization review.
Outlines a repeatable workflow for baseline assessment, quick wins, and ongoing monitoring.
Lists cost optimization checks for budgets, tags, lifecycle policies, and anomaly detection.
limitations: Requires configured AWS CLI credentials and permissions outside the skill.
Does not directly verify whether a resource is safe to delete.
Examples use placeholder resource identifiers that must be replaced by the user.
Savings estimates depend on current AWS pricing, usage patterns, and account context.

use_cases.title: Monthly Cloud Cost Review
use_cases.description: Analyze recent AWS costs by service and compare current spending with previous periods.

use_cases.title: Infrastructure Cleanup Planning
use_cases.description: Identify candidate idle resources and prepare a reviewed cleanup plan with safeguards.

use_cases.title: Leadership Savings Report
use_cases.description: Turn cost findings into prioritized recommendations, expected savings, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Review AWS Spend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-optimizer to review my AWS spending for the last 30 days by service. Explain the main cost drivers and any unusual changes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Quick Wins
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-optimizer to find likely quick wins such as unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, old snapshots, and idle instances. Do not recommend deletion without validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Rightsizing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-optimizer to assess EC2 and RDS utilization using CloudWatch metrics. Recommend rightsizing candidates with risk notes and rollback considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Build FinOps Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use aws-cost-optimizer to create a cost optimization report for leadership. Include trends, top services, prioritized actions, estimated savings, owners, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Analyze AWS costs for the last three months by service.
output_examples.output: A service-level cost summary with trend notes, top spending categories, suspected anomalies, and follow-up AWS CLI checks.

output_examples.input: Find resources that may be safe to clean up.
output_examples.output: A cleanup candidate list grouped by resource type, with validation steps, risk level, and expected cost impact.

output_examples.input: Prepare a leadership cost optimization report.
output_examples.output: A concise report covering spending trend, major drivers, recommended actions, savings range, operational risk, and next review date.

best_practices: Run read-only discovery first and validate ownership before changing resources.
Use tags, backups, and change tickets before deleting or rightsizing production resources.
Compare savings against reliability, performance, and recovery requirements.
anti_patterns: Deleting snapshots, volumes, or instances only because they appear unused.
Using broad administrator credentials for routine cost analysis commands.
Applying rightsizing changes without workload owners and rollback plans.

faq.question: Does this skill require AWS credentials?
faq.answer: Yes. The AWS CLI examples require credentials with permission to read billing, EC2, CloudWatch, RDS, and related data.

faq.question: Can it delete AWS resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples. Users must review and run any cleanup commands themselves.

faq.question: Is it safe for production accounts?
faq.answer: It can support production reviews when used read-only. Any deletion or rightsizing action needs a separate change process.

faq.question: What AWS services does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Cost Explorer, EC2, EBS, Elastic IPs, CloudWatch, RDS, S3, budgets, and anomaly detection.

faq.question: Can it calculate exact savings?
faq.answer: It can help estimate savings, but exact values depend on current pricing, usage, commitments, and account configuration.

faq.question: Does it replace AWS Trusted Advisor?
faq.answer: No. It complements AWS tools by organizing analysis prompts, CLI checks, and reporting workflows.
